steps.title: Apply the factoring from the given condition
Compute the target value
steps.content: Divide by $5$: $x-5 = 2$. That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. Adding the like terms on the left-hand side of the given equation yields $5 \times (x - 5) = 10$. Dividing both sides of this equation by 5 yields $x - 5 = 2$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from subtracting 5, not dividing by 5, on both sides of the equation $5 \times (x - 5) = 10$. Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of x, not the value of $x - 5$. Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $x + 5$, not the value of $x - 5$.
